MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Douglas Applegate appearing by Zoom for Rina Vargas (Petitioner). Randall Boesnnecker appearing by Zoom for W.D. Cox and Company.

The Court stated before it today is a Petition to Determine Right, Title and Interest in Personal Property and a Petition to Determine Right to Title and Possession of Personal Property of Decedent. The Court stated that the property at issue is the same, involves the same facts, and that both seem ready for trial. Mr. Applegate stated that the parties agree to do a trial by affidavit due the current pandemic, that they would open with an affidavit / opening statements in 60 days, and that rebuttal/closing would be done in 90 days. The Court stated that it would continue the petitions, that it would set a Case Management Conference (CMC) for October 20, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that before that date it wanted counsel to submit a CMC statement/case management plan and trial plan. The Court also stated that the CMC order includes the trial date of January 12, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that all dates are set forth in the CMC order. The Court stated that it wants a better idea of how this will be handled and that on the trial date it will decide if the matter will stay in Dept. 204 or be sent out. The Court ordered counsel for W.C. Cox company to download and serve the CMC order once filed. The Petition to Determine Right, Title and Interest in Personal Property and the Petition to Determine Right to Title and Possession of Personal Property of Decedent are continued to October 20, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

The Court stated that there needs to be an objection to the petition, that it is not sure this is a proper 850 petition and that there is a notice issue. Discussion was held as to whether or not 850 applies and whether it meets the criteria. The Petition to Determine Right, Title and Interest in Personal Property is continued to January 29, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of Mr. Boessenecker's objections and for Mr. Applegate to take care of the notice issue and determine if he is going to proceed under Probate Code 850. Mr. Applegate spoke of the notice issue being moot as all Parties made an appearance on the demurrer. The Court noted that there is a June 15, 2020 hearing date on calendar for status of final distribution and encouraged the Parties to talk about resolution.

Demurrer To Petition To Determine Right, Title And Interest In Personal Property
Off Calendar Oct-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
John E. Boessenecker, Esq. for W.C. Cox & Company (assignee), moving party.
Douglas A. Applegate, Esq. for Rina Vargas.
Laura Woodard for Kemp & Associates; attorney-in-fact for eleven heirs.

Matter called, argument held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on W.C. Cox & Company's Demurrer To Petition To Determine Right, Title And Interest In Personal Property as follows: The Demurrer to Petition to Determine Right, Title, and Interest in Personal Property ("the Petition") is overruled. On demurrer, the Court cannot consider an unpleaded settlement agreement, let alone treat it as governing the parties' rights. Nor on demurrer can the Court assume that the Motion to Compel Enforcement of Settlement Agreement is successful. Moreover, even assuming that the moving party's Probate Code 850 petition is still pending, the Petition does not involve identical parties in the same relation to each other, does not seek identical relief, and determination of moving party's petition would not necessarily completely resolve the Petition. Mr. Applegate is to prepare a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and submit it to the Court for review and signature.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: J. Alameda. Deputy: C. Wu. Matter not reported.

Motion To Compel Enforcement Of Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Oct-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
John E. Boessenecker, Esq. for W.C. Cox & Company (assignee), moving party.
Douglas A. Applegate, Esq. for Rina Vargas.
Laura Woodard for Kemp & Associates; attorney-in-fact for eleven heirs.

Matter called, argument held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on W.C. Cox & Company's Motion To Compel Enforcement Of Settlement Agreement as follows: The Motion to Compel Enforcement of Settlement Agreement is denied. In order to take advantage of the special expedited motion procedure provided by Code of Civil Procedure 664.6 the settlement agreement must be signed by the parties themselves. It is not sufficient that the agreement is signed by a litigant's attorney of record. See Levy v. Superior Court (1995) 10 Cal.4th 578, 586; Wackeen v. Malis (2002) 97 Cal.App. 4th 429, 440. In this case, John E Boessenecker, attorney of record for W.C. Cox & Company, signed on behalf of W.C. Cox & Company. Therefore, even assuming that the Court retains jurisdiction over the putatively settled case and that the agreement is otherwise enforceable, section 664.6 is not an available mechanism to enforce it. Mr. Applegate is to prepare a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and submit it to the Court for review and signature.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: J. Alameda. Deputy: C. Wu. Matter not reported.
